title: Nexus extension manifest
description: Schema for the manifest.json document that declares a Nexus extension's identity and all surfaces it contributes to the forum. Mirrors the validation behaviour of Nexus.Extensions.ManifestSchema in the Elixir codebase.

manifest_version.description: Manifest schema version. Currently only version 2 is supported.

name.description: Human-readable name shown to admins and in the extension store.

slug.description: Lowercase, URL-safe identifier. Used as the extension's namespace under /ext/<slug>.

version.description: Semantic version of this manifest (e.g. "1.0.0" or "1.0.0-beta.1").

compatible_with.description: Semver-style constraint on the Nexus version this extension supports (e.g. "^4.0").

module.description: The Elixir module name implementing Nexus.Extensions.Behaviour (e.g. "Gamepedia" or "Gamepedia.Extension").

js_bundle.description: Relative path to the JS bundle within the extension's priv/static directory (e.g. "gamepedia.js"). Must not be absolute or contain '..'.

settings_schema.description: Declarative schema for the admin settings form. See https://docs.nexusprism.org/extensions/building/

settings_tabs.description: Optional tab grouping for the admin settings form.

capabilities.description: Capabilities this extension declares it needs. Enforcement is planned for a future Nexus version; declared values are advisory for now.

side_data.description: Entity attachments this extension contributes. Each entry declares: I attach data of `kind` to entities of `entity`. The compose flow dispatches matching attachments to this extension's persist_attachment/3 callback at create time. Extensions are responsible for cleaning up linked rows when entities are deleted, typically by subscribing to the corresponding *_deleted hook event.

entity.description: The core entity type this side-data attaches to.

kind.description: A string identifier for this kind of attachment. Extensions should namespace their kinds (e.g. "gamepedia_game_link") to avoid collisions with other extensions.

hooks.description: Hook event subscriptions for this extension's handle_event/3 callback. Each entry is either the event name as a string (default priority 50) or an object with explicit priority. Handlers run in priority order at dispatch time, lowest first; within the same priority, order is undefined.

priority.description: Lower runs first. Defaults to 50. Handlers within the same priority have undefined order.

slots.description: UI slot names this extension's JS bundle registers components into.

routes.description: SPA routes this extension owns. Paths are relative to /ext/<slug>; do not include /ext/ yourself.

path.description: Path relative to /ext/<slug>, must start with /.

title.description: Title shown in the page back-header. Optional.

admin_panel.description: Adds an entry to the admin sidebar under 'installed extensions'. The component is registered via the JS bundle.

icon.description: Font Awesome solid icon class, e.g. "fa-gamepad".

explore.description: Adds an entry to the left sidebar Explore section.

path.description: Path within your extension namespace (defaults to /). The Explore item links to /ext/<slug><path>.

digest_sections.description: Sections this extension contributes to the digest email. Each section's data is produced by handle_digest_section/3.

right_widgets.description: Right sidebar widgets this extension's JS bundle registers.

scope.description: Where the widget appears. Defaults to "extension".

toolbar_buttons.description: Composer toolbar buttons this extension's JS bundle registers.

icon.description: Full Font Awesome class with style prefix (e.g. "fa-solid fa-gamepad").

profile_tabs.description: Tabs this extension's JS bundle registers on /profile/:username pages. Each tab has a stable id (used for deep-linking and the initialTab navigation prop), a label shown in the tab bar, and optional icon/visibility/priority. The extension's component is registered separately via the JS bundle's NE.registerProfileTab call.

id.description: Stable identifier for this tab. Used as the URL fragment for deep-linking and must match the id passed to registerProfileTab.

label.description: Display label in the tab bar.

icon.description: Optional Font Awesome solid icon class (e.g. "fa-trophy"). When omitted, only the label shows.

visibility.description: When the tab button appears. "always" (default) shows the tab to anyone viewing the profile; "own_only" shows the tab only when the viewer is the profile owner. This is a UX hint only — it gates the button, not access to underlying data. Enforce access control server-side if needed.

priority.description: Lower values render earlier in the tab bar. Defaults to 50.

notification_types.description: Notification types this extension sends. Each declared type appears as a row in the user's notification preferences page (grouped under a per-extension header) and is used to validate notification data payloads at send time. Send notifications matching these keys via Nexus.Notifications.notify_extension/3 server-side, or POST /api/v1/notifications/extension from the JS bundle.

key.description: Stable identifier sent as data.ext_type. Should be prefixed with the extension slug for clarity (e.g. "gamepedia_new_game").

label.description: Short user-facing name shown in the preferences row (e.g. "New game added").

description.description: Sublabel shown below the label in preferences, explaining what triggers this notification.

icon.description: Optional Font Awesome solid icon class. Defaults to fa-bell.

channels.description: Which delivery channels this notification type supports. The user can independently toggle each. Channels not in this list render as a greyed-out '—' in the preferences row and never deliver.

default_preferences.description: Per-channel default toggle state for new users. Keys must be channels declared above; values are booleans. Defaults to web=true, others=false.

payload_schema.description: Optional contract for the data payload at send time. Keys are field names; values are human-readable descriptions of what each field is for. When present, Nexus validates outgoing notifications of this type against the schema and rejects ones missing declared fields.

permissions.description: Permission gates this extension enforces, surfaced on the admin Permissions page under a per-extension header. Each entry declares a gate by key and label; the admin sets the minimum role required. Check gates server-side with Nexus.Extensions.Permissions.check/3. Values are stored in the extension's settings map, so permission keys must not collide with settings_schema keys.

key.description: Stable identifier used in Permissions.check/3 calls. Lowercase alphanumeric and underscores only. Must not collide with any settings_schema key.

label.description: Human-readable description of what this gate controls, shown as the row label on the Permissions page (e.g. "Can view the gallery").

default.description: The permission level applied before an admin has explicitly saved a value. 'everyone' includes guests (subject to the site-wide guest_browsing setting). Defaults to 'member' if omitted.
